title: Mandatory Compound Compliance State
summary: In dry F1 races, drivers generally must use at least two dry tyre specifications, so late-race cars still on one dry compound have a structural pit obligation.
rationale: In dry F1 races, drivers generally must use at least two dry tyre specifications, so late-race cars still on one dry compound have a structural pit obligation. Current features know compound and stint but not whether the driver has already satisfied the compound rule.
implementation_hint: Within each Year-Race-Driver ordered by LapNumber, build past-only flags/counts: unique dry compounds used so far, current dry compound seen before, has_used_two_dry_compounds, late_race_must_switch proxy, and interactions with laps remaining. Keep wet/intermediate rows separate because dry-compound obligation changes in wet races.
expected_effect: Improves high-probability late-race cases where tyre age alone underestimates required stops.
risk: Dataset may include simulations/testing or wet races where real FIA rule assumptions are imperfect; gate on Race != Pre-Season Testing and dry compounds only.
